text.title: AI Cost Attribution Schema Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for stamping LLM calls with attribution metadata.

rule.statement: Every LLM call carries 8 required tags: tenant_id, feature, route, model, prompt_cache_hit, input_tokens, output_tokens, latency_ms. Missing tag → call rejected (or warns + tagged as "_unknown_").
rule.rationale: Optional tags slip to mostly-empty within weeks; required tags force the team to design the call site to know its identity.

rule.statement: Tags are stamped by a single middleware, not by per-call kwargs. Middleware reads tenant_id and feature from the request context, not from caller arguments.
rule.rationale: Per-call kwargs drift; one missed `feature=` arg in a new route silently breaks attribution. Middleware enforcement is unbypassable.

rule.statement: tenant_id is a stable UUID/slug — never "team", "us", "unknown", or an email at the application layer. If caller cannot supply a tenant, it is anonymous="anon_session_{request_id_hash}".
rule.rationale: Generic strings collapse the slice and the dashboard shows half the bill under "unknown".

rule.statement: Cost (USD) is computed at write time using the model-pricing snapshot at that moment, and stored as a column. Never recomputed on read against current pricing.
rule.rationale: Vendor pricing changes; recomputing makes historical reports drift; write-time snapshots are append-only truth.

rule.statement: Attribution captures 100% of calls (no sampling). Sampling is acceptable only for body content in traces, never for the cost record itself.
rule.rationale: Sampled cost records cannot be reconciled against the vendor invoice; 100% capture is the only auditable position.

rule.statement: A daily aggregator emits a per-(date, tenant, feature, model) table; a dashboard shows the top dimensions and an invoice-reconciliation column (sum of computed cost vs invoice total).
rule.rationale: Without the reconciliation column the team trusts the dashboard while the schema silently misses 20% of calls.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire middleware + aggregator + dashboard per the procedure.
Bill below $100/mo or single-feature single-tenant → skip; attribution overhead exceeds insight.
